layman_headline: Bitcoin is rallying to one-month highs but altcoins look exhausted — patience is the play.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin just hit $66,300 with five straight days of ETF inflows, and the broader market is bouncing. However, most altcoin candidates on our watchlist have already moved 20-50% in the past week, meaning the easy money has been made. The overall market mood is still in 'Extreme Fear' at 25/100, which can be a contrarian buy signal, but Bitcoin dominance at 56.8% tells us money is flowing into BTC, not alts. Chasing extended altcoins here carries significant pullback risk.
layman_action: Wait for a pullback before entering any new position. The best setups right now have already run hard. If ETH dips back toward $1,840, that could be a clean entry — otherwise, stay in cash and watch.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all 9 candidates, the problem is clear: the coins with the best chart patterns (LDO +0.85 bias, UB +0.80, 币安人生 +0.80) have already surged 27-40% in 7 days, making entries at current levels a chase with poor risk/reward ratios (LDO and ONDO both show 0.38x R:R). DEXE shows extreme data inconsistency — price listed at $14.72 but chart says $36.27, with RSI at 71 (overbought) despite a -64% weekly drop — this is untradeable noise. ETH stands out as the highest-quality asset with RSI at 39 (our most reliable signal at 83% win rate), but the chart bias is -0.70 (bearish structure, below all MAs) and it's already bounced 7.6% this week. The prudent move is to wait for a retest of the $1,800-1,840 support zone before entering, rather than chasing the current bounce in a fearful market with high BTC dominance suppressing alts.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.8% continues to drain capital from alts — ETH could underperform even if BTC rallies further
Extreme Fear (25) can persist or deepen, meaning the bottom may not be in yet for altcoins
Oil near $90 creates macro headwinds; if risk-off sentiment returns, the current rally could reverse sharply
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,800-1,840 support — a hold there with RSI dipping below 35 would be an ideal entry
Bitcoin ETF inflow streak — if it breaks, the rally narrative weakens and alts will sell off harder
BTC dominance trend — a reversal below 56% would signal capital rotating back into alts
market_context: Bitcoin is at a one-month high of $66.3K driven by sustained institutional ETF inflows and options positioning, but the broader market remains in Extreme Fear (25/100). High BTC dominance at 56.8% means altcoins are underperforming, making this a BTC-led rally that hasn't yet lifted the alt ecosystem.
context_banner: Bitcoin breaks to one-month highs on institutional flows, but altcoin setups are overextended — discipline demands patience over FOMO.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold — our most reliable signal (83% win rate) — but needs to dip further or consolidate before entry is optimal.
Extreme Fear at 25/100 is historically a contrarian buy zone (67% win rate in our trades), but the flat trend suggests fear hasn't capitulated yet.
Bitcoin ETFs posting 5 consecutive days of inflows confirms institutional demand is real, supporting the broader rally thesis.
BTC breakout to $66.3K and Russian crypto regulation news suggest macro-level adoption catalysts are building — reliable source at 67% win rate.

Best-scoring chart patterns (LDO Three White Soldiers at +0.85, UB Morning Star at +0.80) are on coins already up 27-40% weekly with poor risk/reward ratios of 0.38x — not actionable at current prices.
